name: Fish Portuguese
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon olive oil or vegetable oil
2 pounds fish fillets (flounder, cod, turbot, etc.), cut into bite-size pieces
shrimp (raw), shelled and deveined, cut into halves
2 cans Italian tomatoes, or 4 fresh tomatoes, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Add diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
3. Add fish fillets to the pot and cook for 4-5 minutes on each side until lightly browned.
4. Stir in minced clams, crab meat (if using), shrimp, and scallops.
5. Add chopped tomatoes, garlic, parsley, diced carrots, potatoes, string beans, salt, pepper, cayenne pepper, Worcestershire sauce, saffron, and bay leaf.
6. If using fresh tomatoes, add 1/2 cup water. Do not add water if using canned tomatoes.
7. Cover the pot and simmer on low heat for 20-25 minutes, or until fish is cooked through.
8. Remove crab meat (if using) from the pot during the last 3 minutes of cooking to prevent overcooking.
9. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
10. Serve hot with garlic bread. Garnish with corn, celery, and leaves if desired.
